reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: Across the last three games, almost all initial answers have been correct (only 1–2 wrong out of 12), so each other player’s capsule is likely highly reliable. That makes the probability that all three remaining answers are correct reasonably high. If I refuse to volunteer, past patterns suggest no one else will volunteer in Round 1, exposing me to a 25% random-death chance immediately, plus further random-death risk in later rounds. On balance, accepting the (likely small) risk that one of the other answers is wrong in exchange for avoiding guaranteed random-elimination risk seems better, and if the password is correct I also keep all 4 coins.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q1  
REASONING: Volunteering now would mean trusting three completely unverified answers (Q1, Q3, Q4), which is too risky even though I know my own Q2 is correct. If no one volunteers this round, there will be a random death whether I pass or verify, so verifying strictly dominates passing: same survival risk but more information for future rounds. I’ll verify Q1 (arbitrarily among the non-mine questions) to start building consensus.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q4  
REASONING: Volunteering now would require trusting four completely unverified capsules, which is unnecessarily risky—one wrong answer kills the volunteer. Passing has the same random-death risk as verifying if nobody volunteers, but gives no information. Spending 1 coin to verify another question is therefore the best use of this round.

I already know my own puzzle (Q3: 12×5=60) is correct, so verifying Q3 would add no value. Among the others, D’s Q4 is the least trusted, since D has produced a wrong answer in a previous game, while A and B have been consistently reliable so far. Verifying Q4-D gives the highest information gain, helping us isolate any weak link in the password and enabling a safer, informed volunteer decision in later rounds.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
🔍 Verifications completed:
  ✅ B agrees with Q1-A → now Q1-A+B (-1 coin: 4→3)
  ✅ C agrees with Q4-D → now Q4-D+C (-1 coin: 4→3)

✅ A entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A+B, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D+C
🎉 Everyone escapes! 🎉
